title: quercus robur subsp. longipeduncula
intro: Quercus robur subsp. longipeduncula is a subspecies of the English oak tree, characterized by its distinctive long stalks supporting the acorns. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Quercus robur subsp. longipeduncula include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to calm the digestive system and promote overall well-being.
